title: Immunotherapy + chemo — PFS vs OS
notes: Synthetic-but-realistic. Ground truth is controlled by pairing each claim with an authored reference snippet. VERIFY/expand before publishing as a benchmark.
manuscript: In the PRISM-3 trial, pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy improved progression-free survival compared with chemotherapy alone (median 9.2 vs 5.1 months; HR 0.52, 95% CI 0.41-0.65).1 Overall survival was significantly prolonged with the combination.1 The aim of this analysis was to describe long-term outcomes.

goldClaims.originalText: pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy improved progression-free survival compared with chemotherapy alone (median 9.2 vs 5.1 months; HR 0.52, 95% CI 0.41-0.65).1
goldClaims.text: pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy improved progression-free survival compared with chemotherapy alone (median 9.2 vs 5.1 months; HR 0.52, 95% CI 0.41-0.65)

goldClaims.text: Overall survival was significantly prolonged with the combination

goldNonClaims: The aim of this analysis was to describe long-term outcomes.

1.name: PRISM-3 primary (Doe 2024)
1.text: In the PRISM-3 study, pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy significantly improved progression-free survival versus chemotherapy alone, with a median of 9.2 months compared with 5.1 months (hazard ratio 0.52; 95% CI 0.41-0.65; p<0.001). Overall survival data were immature at the time of this analysis and the difference between arms did not reach statistical significance.

goldVerdicts.claimText: pembrolizumab plus chemotherapy improved progression-free survival compared with chemotherapy alone (median 9.2 vs 5.1 months; HR 0.52, 95% CI 0.41-0.65)
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref states the PFS result with matching figures and p<0.001 explicitly.

goldVerdicts.claimText: Overall survival was significantly prolonged with the combination
goldVerdicts.rationale: Ref says OS was immature and did not reach significance — negates 'significantly prolonged' (rule 1).
